title: The VALIS Trilogy — Page 462
raw_text:
could an intelligent, educated man, a great man, really, one of the most
powerful men of his times—how could he begin to believe in that?” Bishop
Archer said. “It is a mystery to me. It is an enigma that perhaps will never be
solved.”
I saw how tired he was, so I got my coat and purse, said good night, and
departed.
My car had been ticketed. Shit, I said to myself as I pulled the ticket from
the wiper-blade and stuck it into my pocket. While we’re reading Goethe,
Lovely Rita Meter-Maid is ticketing my car. What a strange world, I thought;
or, rather, strange worlds—plural. They do not come together.
